NO LATER THAN JANUARY 9, 2026 DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A RESPONSIVE DECLARATION (NOT TO EXCEED 2 PAGES).JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)
REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE (TRANSACTION ID # 78550210) FILED BY DEFENDANT ZHENG, LANFANG , AN INDIVIDUAL KAO, COLEMAN , AN INDIVIDUAL

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION TO APPOINT BROKER PURSUANT TO PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY RULED THAT PLAINTIFF MAY EITHER PROCEED WITH A PARTITION IN KIND OR CLEARLY START THE PROCESS FOR PARTITION BY SALE, WHICH UNDER THE PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT START WITH AN UNEQUIVOCAL DEMAND FOR PARTITION BY SALE; PROCEEDS WITH CCP 874.316 AND 874.317 PROCEDURES; AND ONLY THEREAFTER MAY RESULT IN THE SALE ON THE MARKET NECESSITATING THE HIRING OF A BROKER. BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS, PARTITION IN KIND IS NOT REQUESTED (MOVING PAPERS 2:15-17); AND, BASED ON THE REPLY PARTITION BY SALE IS ALSO NOT REQUESTED (REPLY 3:18). IN THE ABSENCE OF A REQUEST FOR PARTITION IN KIND, THE COURT MAY NOT ORDER SUCH RELIEF; IN THE ABSENCE OF A REQUEST FOR PARTITION BY SALE THE COURT MAY NOT ORDER SUCH RELIEF EITHER; THEREFORE, THE ONLY OPTION REMAINING TO THE COURT UNDER CCP 874.318(B) IS TO DISMISS THE ACTION. PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER INITIATING THE BUYOUT PERIOD UPON DETERMINING PROPERTY VALUE NOTIFICATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-07-2026 Set For Trial On Oct-26-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Broker Pursuant To Partition Of Real Property Act
Off Calendar Mar-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Arthur Duggan, Esq. representing Plaintiff Yuan Zhou et al via Zoom Video Webinar.
Benjamin Tagert, Esq. representing Defendant Lanfang Zheng et al via Zoom Video Webinar.

Discussion and arguments made.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling with modification as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' Motion To Appoint Broker Pursuant To Partition Of Real Property Act

is DENIED. The Court has previously ruled that Plaintiff may either proceed with a partition in kind or clearly start the process for partition by sale, which under the partition of Real Property Act start with an unequivocal demand for partition by sale; proceeds with CCP 874.316 and 874.317 procedures; and only thereafter may result in the sale on the market necessitating the hiring of a broker. Based on Plaintiff's moving papers, partition in kind is not requested (Moving papers 2:15-17); and, based on the reply Partition by sale is also not requested (Reply 3:18). In the absence of a request for partition in kind, the Court may not order such relief; in the absence of a request for partition by sale the Court may not order such relief either; therefore, the only option remaining to the Court under CCP 874.318(b) is to dismiss the action.
Parties are to meet and confer and submit a proposed order initiating the buyout period upon determining property value notification.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Re: Referee Appointment

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO APPOINT BROKER PURSUANT TO PARTITION OF REAL PROPERTY ACT AND TO APPOINT REFEREE TO ISSUE REPORT ON OFFSETS

is DENIED without prejudice for Plaintiff to bring a motion for appointment of referee, if necessary and proper, after Plaintiff locates a proposed referee ready and willing to accomplish the partition in-kind envisioned by Plaintiff. Alternatively, Plaintiff may proceed with the proper procedure for partition by sale.
